Round 7 restructured the sealed wf checker's report-entry granularity (per-finding, so the batch footer "WARNING: N wellformedness check failed!" count matches the reference) without changing any rendered block byte.
RULES: mechanical re-sync only (documented recipe: crate::->super:: path fixes, preserve crates/tamarin-parser/src/wf/order.rs + its mod-lines); clean files stay headerless; do NOT hand-edit sealed code; no git commits; PATH needs /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin.

Check whether open-side callers (run.rs wf_report handling, pretty_theory format_wf_block / wf_headerless_preamble grouping) need adjustment for the new granularity — the BLOCK output must stay byte-identical while wf_report.len() becomes the per-finding count. Open-side adjustments are yours to make; document them.
2. cargo build --release; cargo test -p tamarin-parser -p tamarin-theory -p tamarin-prover -p tamarin-server.
3. Full wf gate: RESULTS_TSV=scripts/wf_gate_round7.tsv JOBS=6 bash scripts/wf_gate.sh — MUST stay 419 MATCH / 0 DIFF.

Footer gate: printf '%s\n' 'features/auto-sources/tamarin-repo/sapic/statVerifLeftRight/stateverif_left_right.spthy' 'regression/trace/issue515.spthy' 'regression/trace/issue527.spthy' 'sapic/deprecated/accountability-old/CertificateTransparency.spthy' 'sapic/deprecated/accountability-old/OCSPS.spthy' > /tmp/wf_r7_allow.txt; then ALLOWLIST=/tmp/wf_r7_allow.txt RESULTS_TSV=scripts/corpus_diff_r7_footer.tsv JOBS=4 FILE_TIMEOUT=600 bash scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh — expect 5 MATCH / 0 DIFF (expected footer counts 3/14/14/5/6). Also spot-check 3 previously-MATCH warning-carrying files' footers stayed correct.
5. scripts/gen_license_headers.py --check (0 stale; no wf/ file gained a header).
Append a dated round-7 section to INTEGRATION_REPORT.md. Return a plain-text summary with exact numbers. Keep-and-report anything divergent.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

The clean 34 crates' own suites still pass at their workspace locations: `graph-clean` 16 35 round-trip + 7 abbrev + 1 doctest; `web-clean` parity suite. The vendored 36 `web_clean` inline unit tests (19) run and pass inside `tamarin-server`. 37 38 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 39 ## 1. WEB cluster — REWIRED (partial, byte-safe) 40 41 Adapters route prover-generated content as opaque pane/body strings into the 42 clean templates. All routed paths are byte-identical to the ported output for 43 the corpus/fixture theories, or a strictly-more-HS-faithful byte change that 44 the (structural) parity suite accepts. 45 46 REPLACED (ported render logic removed, now sourced from the clean layer): 47 48 * `handlers/theory.rs::intdot` — the ported `intdotLayout` `format!` template 49 deleted; now `web_clean::intdot::render_intdot(&html_escape(name), &dotsrc)`. 50 Byte-identical (the `` prefix `Theory: ` has no escapable chars, so 51 escaping `Theory: NAME` vs escaping `NAME` coincide). 52 * `handlers/theory_html.rs::overview_page` — for **local-origin** theories the 53 whole page shell now comes from `web_clean::page::render_page`, with the 54 west (proof-script state) and center (main-view HTML) panes passed as opaque 55 strings (`"{pane} "` — the trailing space the ported template emitted). Byte- 56 identical to the ported local output (verified head/nav/pane/tail against 57 `shell_template`). 58 * `handlers/theory_html.rs` add/delete forms — `add_lemma_html` and 59 `delete_lemma_html` deleted; `path_html` now calls 60 `web_clean::forms::add_form` / `delete_form`. Byte change: the clean forms 61 reproduce HS's stray `</span>` in the `<noscript>` block (byte-exact HS), 62 which the ported code had normalized away. Structural suite unaffected. 63 64 REMAINS PORTED (kept, headers untouched) — with the precise blocker: 65 66 * `overview_page` **non-local** branch: `web_clean::page`'s shell bakes in the 67 local-origin header (Reload-file / Append-modified-lemmas actions); a 68 non-local (uploaded) origin gates those off, which the clean shell does not 69 model. Routing it would add the two actions — a behavior change — so the 70 ported template is kept for that branch only. 71 * `edit_lemma_html`: `web_clean::forms::edit_form` hard-codes the textarea 72 `rows="8"`; HS sizes it dynamically (`textHeight = 2 + #newlines` in the 73 lemma plaintext). Routing Edit through the clean form would drop that. Gap in 74 `web_clean::forms` (a fixed `rows` slot); kept ported. 75 * `proof_state` (west pane) and the `path_html` main-view bodies (help / 76 message / rules / sources / proof / tactic) stay in the workspace: these ARE 77 the opaque prover content the clean shell consumes — the prover pretty-prints 78 (via `tamarin_theory`) are data the adapter supplies, not scaffolding. 79 `theory_html.rs` is therefore NOT deleted; it is now the pane/body supplier + 80 the two kept branches above. 81 * JSON envelopes (`handlers/json_resp.rs`) and the 404 page: `web_clean:: 82 envelope` (`{html,title}` / `{redirect}`) and `web_clean::errors:: 83 render_not_found` are validated and byte-compatible on keys, BUT the ported 84 `json_resp` returns `axum::Json<Value>` across ~40 call sites (and carries a 85 third `{alert}` variant `web_clean` does not model), and `not_found_response` 86 is a pervasive minimal-body stub with no request-path in scope. Rewiring 87 either is pure return-type/plumbing churn with no behavior win under the 88 structural gate; left for a follow-up (noted, not forced). 89 90 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 91 ## 2. GRAPH/DOT cluster — VENDORED; serializer rewire BLOCKED, reported 92 93 `graph_clean` is vendored, builds, and passes its round-trip corpus. The DOT 94 **pipeline rewire** (`handlers/dot.rs` `system_to_dot` / `system_to_dot_with` 95 / `render_svg_or_dot_with` -> repr/simplify -> adapter -> graph_clean model -> 96 graph_clean abbrev -> graph_clean serialization) is **not** performed: it cannot 97 be a thin adapter without a behavior change, blocked by three concrete gaps 98 between `graph_clean`'s model and the payload the ported serializer produces. 99 Per protocol ("where the clean crate lacks a behavior, leave the old path in 100 place and report the gap; never patch clean code with old-code logic"), the 101 ported `handlers/dot.rs` and `graph/abbreviation.rs` are kept intact (headers 102 untouched). `graph/abbreviation.rs` is therefore NOT deleted. 103 104 Blockers (each independently fatal to a byte-faithful, thin-adapter rewire): 105 106 1. **Term rendering / abbreviation are one coupled behavior the clean crate 107 deliberately omits.** `graph_clean` treats record-cell text as pre-rendered 108 (BEHAVIOR.md §3a `[GAP]`: full solver term pretty-printer, incl. the 109 `renderRow`/`renderBalanced` width-proportional record-row wrapping, is out 110 of scope), and its `abbrev` names/substitutes over its OWN independent 111 `term::Term` model. The ported cell text is rendered from `LNTerm` with the 112 HS-faithful balanced wrapping AND abbreviations substituted in-place 113 (`apply_abbreviations_fact`, `LNTerm`-keyed). `graph_clean::abbrev` cannot 114 substitute a chosen name into `LNTerm`-rendered text; using it for selection 115 would force routing all term rendering through `graph_clean::Term`, losing 116 the balanced wrapping — exactly the behavior the protocol says to keep. So 117 both the cell renderer and `graph/abbreviation.rs` must stay ported. 118 119 2. **No representation for missing-node shapes.** `graph_clean::model::NodeKind` 120 is `Record | Ellipse | Plain`, and `Ellipse` emits `shape="ellipse"` only. 121 HS "missing" nodes are `trapezium` / `invtrapezium` (`dotConcC`/`dotPremC`). 122 A constraint system whose edges reference an absent node cannot be modeled; 123 extending the model would be patching clean code (forbidden). 124 125 3. **HS node/port-id allocation is not modeled.** `graph_clean` takes node ids 126 and record ports verbatim (its round-trip builds them from captured DOT); 127 it does not generate HS's graph-global `<n0>,<n1>,…` port scheme (`Text.Dot` 128 `cacheState`/`dsPrems`/`dsConcs`). Reproducing byte-exact HS ids requires 129 that allocation logic, which lives in the ported serializer being "deleted" 130 — writing it into the adapter would transplant replaced logic. 131 132 Consequence: a rewire that kept the ported cell renderer + abbreviation + 133 missing-node fallback + id scheme would GROW `handlers/dot.rs` (adapter on top 134 of a still-live serializer) rather than thin it, and would not be byte-faithful 135 — the opposite of the task's goal. Recommended: a `graph_clean` round-2 that 136 (a) adds arbitrary `shape=` support (trapezium), (b) exposes an HS port/node-id 137 allocator, and (c) either a `LNTerm`->`Term` bridge or an accepted term-render 138 GAP; then the thin-adapter rewire becomes possible. One isolated sub-component 139 IS cleanly routable today — the legend table via `graph_clean::abbrev:: 140 legend_html` (byte-exact, incl. the 65-space hang indent the ported legend 141 omits) — but wiring only it into the otherwise-kept ported serializer mixes two 142 escaping/whitespace regimes for no net structural gain, so it is left for the 143 round-2 rewire. 144 145 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 146 ## 3. LOC delta 147 148 * Added (vendored clean sources, headerless): `graph_clean` 837 + `web_clean` 149 1005 = **1842 LOC**. 150 * Adapter glue authored in kept files (headerless additions within headered 151 files): ~35 LOC (theory.rs intdot call, overview_page local branch, 152 path_html arms, lib.rs module decls). 153 * Deleted (GPL-headered ported render logic): `add_lemma_html` (~22) + 154 `delete_lemma_html` (~21) + ported `intdot` `format!` template (~15) ≈ **58 155 LOC**. Tracked-file diff: +82 / -70 across `theory.rs`, `theory_html.rs`, 156 `lib.rs`. 157 * Not deleted (blocked/kept, reported above): `handlers/dot.rs` (2266), 158 `graph/abbreviation.rs` (whole), `theory_html.rs` non-local + edit + pane 159 suppliers. 160 161 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 162 ## 4. Validation (all green) 163 164 * `cargo build --workspace` — 0 errors. 165 * `cargo test -p tamarin-server` — lib 80 (incl. 19 vendored `web_clean`), 166 routes_autoprove 6, routes_basic 19, routes_graph 4, routes_proof_step 3, 167 routes_static 3, routes_stubs 15, routes_upload 3; doctest 1 ignored. 0 168 failures. The captured-HS-response parity fixtures (routes_basic JSON-key + 169 structural, routes_stubs) stay green. 170 * `cargo test -p tamarin-parser` — all green (67 + 2). 171 * Graph fixture tests (`graph-clean` crate): 16 round-trip + 7 abbrev + 1 172 doctest, 0 failures. Vendored copy verified byte-identical modulo path fixes. 173 174 ================================================================================ 175 # Dirty-room integration report — units C, D, E, F, G 176 177 Date: 2026-07-17. Integrator: dirty-room (adapters only; no logic transplanted 178 from replaced files into clean code). Same protocol as above. 179 180 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 181 ## C. Wellformedness round-3 — RE-SYNCED (part 1) + KEEP-AND-REPORT (part 2) 182 183 ### C.1 Re-sync of clean sources into `crates/tamarin-parser/src/wf/` — DONE 184 Re-applied the established mechanical path-fix recipe (`crate::{pretty,report, 185 formula,checks}` -> `super::…`; `crate::ast` kept, it resolves to the real 186 tamarin-parser AST) to the round-3 clean sources: 187 188 * `wf/checks.rs` <- wf-clean/src/checks.rs (grew 46.7K->61.2K: adds 189 `fact_capitalization`, `formula_terms_reducible`/`formula_terms`, the reducible 190 de-Bruijn term renderer, the round-3 guardedness two modes + wrapped printer). 191 * `wf/formula.rs` <- wf-clean/src/formula.rs (adds `pp_formula_wrapped`, the 192 HughesPJ-style multi-line layout engine). 193 * `wf/pretty.rs`, `wf/report.rs` <- byte-identical clean sources (only 194 `crate::ast`, kept). 195 * `wf/mod.rs` rebuilt from wf-clean/src/lib.rs with the two transforms 196 (`pub mod ast;` dropped; `pub use ast::*;` -> `pub use crate::ast::*;`) and the 197 PRESERVED workspace lines `pub mod order; pub use order::*;` re-appended. 198 `wf/order.rs` untouched (workspace-authored). 199 200 Validation: `cargo test -p tamarin-parser` green (67 + 2 wellformedness); oracle 201 harness `wellformedness_fixtures` = 21/21 parsed / Rust-wf-match / Tamarin-match 202 (100%). Downstream `cargo build -p tamarin-theory -p tamarin-server` clean. 203 204 ### C.2 check_terms.rs / check_guarded_wf residue — KEPT, gaps reported 205 The task's target rewire (route `check_terms::check_terms_wf`'s formula-terms 206 through `wf::formula_terms_reducible`, and the `elaborate::check_guarded_wf` call 207 sites through the clean two-mode guardedness) is NOT performed: both clean 208 entry points have an ORACLE-CONFIRMED behavior gap that a thin adapter cannot 209 close without transplanting the replaced sort-kind logic. Per protocol the 210 ported paths are kept with their GPL-pending headers and the gaps reported. 211 212 Blocker 1 — formula-terms variable binding (PROVEN divergence). The clean 213 `formula_terms_reducible` binds a variable use to a quantifier by NAME ONLY 214 (`checks::debruijn_index`, BEHAVIOR.md "round2 fix"). That is correct for 215 Msg-vs-Untagged and temporal `#i`-vs-`i` collisions but WRONG when a use's 216 sort-KIND differs from a same-named binder. Direct oracle probe (HS v1.13.0 217 binary, wf_oracle.sh) on 218 lemma L: "All #x. (K(x) @ #x) ==> F" 219 prints `Lemma `L' uses terms of the wrong form: `Free x'` 220 because the node binder `#x` does NOT bind the message-position use `x`. The 221 clean name-only model binds it (`Bound 0`) and reports NO offender — a silent 222 regression. The ported `check_terms.rs` matches by name AND sort-kind AND idx 223 (`lookup_bound`/`kind_of`) and reproduces the oracle byte-exactly; its unit test 224 `untagged_message_use_does_not_bind_to_node_binder` is the captured case and 225 stays green. The gap is intrinsic to the clean detection algorithm (baked into 226 `debruijn_index`), so it cannot be split out into an adapter. 227 228 Blocker 2 — guardedness fidelity. The clean `formula_guardedness` is an admitted 229 HEURISTIC over-approximation (BEHAVIOR.md "Guardedness ALGORITHM depth: ∃ failure 230 sub-modes and exotic ∀ bodies beyond the probed cases" is a listed gap; guard 231 variables also matched by NAME only), and it only iterates LEMMAS. The ported 232 `elaborate::check_guarded_wf` drives the REAL semantic conversion 233 `formula_to_guarded` (the solver machinery, which stays ported regardless) and 234 renders via the ported `pretty_formula`; it is byte-exact in the run pipeline. 235 Routing the run.rs/theory_io.rs call sites through the documented-incomplete 236 heuristic risks exactly the captured-HS byte-parity the task requires to stay 237 green, with no faithful fallback available. (Restriction guardedness is not a 238 real divergence either way: an unguarded restriction is a FATAL HS error at 239 Guarded.hs:526, verified by oracle, so it never reaches the wf report.) 240 241 Consequence: `check_terms.rs` is NOT deleted; `check_guarded_wf` and its two call 242 sites (`run.rs`, `theory_io.rs`) are unchanged. The round-3 clean checks DO ship 243 in the parser-level `wf::check_theory` (pre-elaboration, fixture-validated). A 244 future close would need the wf crate to carry the sort-kind binding distinction 245 and a semantic (formula_to_guarded-equivalent) guardedness decision; then the 246 post-elaboration rewire becomes byte-faithful. 247 248 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 249 ## D. Console (cli-clean) — VENDORED + help/version ROUTED; parse/framing KEPT 250 251 Vendored `console/workspace/cli-clean/src` into `crates/tamarin-prover/src/cli/` 252 (suffix-free, headerless) with the mechanical `crate::` -> `super::` fix and the 253 `include_str!` fixture path `../fixtures/` -> `fixtures/` (fixtures copied to 254 `cli/fixtures/`): `modes.rs`, `parse.rs`, `help.rs`, `version.rs`, `framing.rs`, 255 `errors.rs`. The ported `cli.rs` became `cli/mod.rs` (kept, GPL header intact). 256 Workspace adapter authored: `cli/adapt.rs` (headerless). 257 258 ROUTED through the clean layer (ported bodies deleted): 259 * `--help`: `cli/mod.rs::help_text()` body deleted; `adapt::help_text(&sub)` maps 260 the ported `Subcommand` -> clean `Mode` and returns `help::render_help(mode)`. 261 `run.rs` (show_help), `main.rs` (parse-error fallback), and `run.rs`'s batch 262 "no input files" envelope now emit the clean per-mode HS help page. SPOT-CHECK: 263 `tamarin-prover --help` is now BYTE-IDENTICAL to the HS capture 264 (`cli/fixtures/help_global.txt`) — the ported help was a Rust-port-relabeled 265 page ("...verification (Rust port).") that diverged from HS. 266 * `--version`: `version_text()` + `version_maude_stderr_text()` bodies deleted; 267 `adapt::version_stdout()` fills the clean `version.tmpl` slots from this 268 binary's build metadata (`VERSION`/`GIT_REV`/`GIT_BRANCH`/`BUILD_TIMESTAMP` + 269 detected Maude version) and `run.rs` prints it to stdout. SPOT-CHECK: the 270 static template is byte-identical to the HS `--version` capture (dynamic git/ 271 compile/maude slots carry this build's own values); the ported code emitted a 272 DIFFERENT (banner-then-maude, non-interleaved) form. 273 Two ported unit tests (`version_stdout_*` / `version_stderr_*`) that pinned the 274 removed stream-split behavior were deleted; `adapt.rs` carries replacement 275 byte-parity tests against the vendored fixtures. 276 277 KEPT ported (headers intact) — gaps reported: 278 * Arg parsing (`parse_args` + typed `Args`/`Subcommand` + value validation). The 279 clean `parse`/`modes` flag tables model only the HS flag set; this binary adds 280 Rust-specific flags the clean tables do NOT list — `--processors`, 281 `--maude-processes`, `--data-dir` — and the clean `parse` returns an untyped 282 `(name,value)` flag list with no int-parsing / typed defaults / range errors 283 (`bound: invalid bound given`, etc.). Routing tokenization through clean would 284 reject the Rust flags ("Unknown flag") and drop typed validation; per protocol 285 the ported typed parser is kept. (The clean `parse`/`errors` modules are 286 vendored and available for a future pre-strip adapter.) 287 * Batch output framing (`run.rs` maude preamble / `[Theory X]` progress markers / 288 `summary of summaries` block). The ported driver SPLITS streams (progress + 289 preamble -> stderr, summary -> stdout) and emits an aligned `output:` column 290 the clean `framing` (a single merged-capture string, no `output:` slot, no 291 stream model) does not reproduce. 1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

1	//! wf-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover wellformedness
2	//! checker, derived purely from black-box oracle behavior. See
3	//! workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the inferred behavioral spec.
4	
5	pub mod ast;
6	pub mod checks;
7	pub mod formula;
8	pub mod pretty;
9	pub mod report;
10	
11	pub use ast::*;
12	pub use report::{
13	    insert_wf_before, render_report, topics, underline_topic, WfError, WfReport, SUCCESS_LINE,
14	};
15	
16	/// Topics that follow the public-names report in the canonical order; the
17	/// public-names report is inserted immediately before the first of these that
18	/// is present in the report. (Unbound variables and Fresh public constants
19	/// precede the insertion point and are therefore NOT anchors.)
20	pub fn after_public_names_topics() -> Vec<&'static str> {
21	    vec![
22	        checks::T_SORTS,
23	        checks::T_RESERVED,
24	        checks::T_RESERVED_PREFIX,
25	        checks::T_FR,
26	        checks::T_SPECIAL,
27	        checks::T_FACT_CAP,
28	        checks::T_ARITY,
29	        checks::T_MULT,
30	        checks::T_LHSRHS,
31	        checks::T_LEFT,
32	        checks::T_RIGHT,
33	        checks::T_QUANT_SORTS,
34	        checks::T_FORMULA_TERMS,
35	        checks::T_GUARD,
36	        checks::T_LEMMA_ANNOT,
37	        checks::T_MULRESTRICT,
38	        checks::T_NAT,
39	        checks::T_SUBTERM,
40	    ]
41	}
42	
43	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order. The "Formula terms" check runs
44	/// in free-variable-only mode; for reducible-function coverage use
45	/// [`check_theory_with_reducible`].
46	pub fn check_theory(thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
47	    check_theory_with_reducible(thy, &std::collections::BTreeSet::new())
48	}
49	
50	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order, passing a caller-computed set
51	/// of reducible function-symbol names to the "Formula terms" check (theories
52	/// with `equations:` make some user functions reducible; the caller derives
53	/// that set - typically from the equation theory / Maude - and this pipeline
54	/// only consumes it).
55	pub fn check_theory_with_reducible(
56	    thy: &Theory,
57	    reducible: &std::collections::BTreeSet<String>,
58	) -> WfReport {
59	    let mut report: WfReport = Vec::new();
60	    // Base pipeline (public names inserted afterwards at its anchor point).
61	    report.extend(checks::unbound_variables(thy)); // 1
62	    report.extend(checks::fresh_public_constants(thy)); // 2
63	    report.extend(checks::mismatching_sorts(thy)); // 4
64	    report.extend(checks::reserved_names(thy)); // 5
65	    report.extend(checks::reserved_prefixes(thy)); // 6 (diff mode only)
66	    report.extend(checks::fr_facts(thy)); // 7
67	    report.extend(checks::special_facts(thy)); // 8
68	    report.extend(checks::fact_capitalization(thy)); // 8b (precedes arity)
69	    report.extend(checks::fact_arity(thy)); // 9
70	    report.extend(checks::fact_multiplicity(thy)); // 10
71	    report.extend(checks::fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs(thy)); // 11
72	    report.extend(checks::diff_left_right(thy)); // 12, 13 (diff mode only)
73	    // The Quantifier-sorts / Formula-terms / Formula-guardedness bundle is
74	    // emitted per formula item (lemmas then restrictions), interleaved by topic.
75	    report.extend(checks::formula_reports(thy, reducible)); // 14, 15, 16
76	    report.extend(checks::lemma_annotations(thy)); // 17
77	    report.extend(checks::multiplication_restriction(thy)); // 17
78	    report.extend(checks::nat_sorts(thy)); // 18
79	    report.extend(checks::subterm_convergence(thy)); // 19
80	
81	    let pn = checks::public_names_report(thy); // 3
82	    let anchors = after_public_names_topics();
83	    insert_wf_before(&mut report, pn, &anchors);
84	    report
85	}
86	
87	/// Secondary entry point: report any of `lemma_names` that are not declared in
88	/// the theory. NOTE: this entry point's exact render string could not be
89	/// observed through the file oracle; the set-difference logic is verified but
90	/// the message text is a documented gap (see BEHAVIOR.md).
91	pub fn check_if_lemmas_in_theory(lemma_names: &[String], thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
92	    let present = checks::theory_lemma_names(thy);
93	    let mut out = Vec::new();
94	    for name in lemma_names {
95	        if !present.contains(name) {
96	            out.push(WfError::new(
97	                "Lemmas",
98	                format!("lemma `{}' referenced but not present in theory", name),
99	            ));
100	        }
101	    }
102	    out
103	}
104

1	//! wf-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover wellformedness
2	//! checker, derived purely from black-box oracle behavior. See
3	//! workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the inferred behavioral spec.
4	
5	pub mod checks;
6	pub mod formula;
7	pub mod pretty;
8	pub mod report;
9	
10	pub use crate::ast::*;
11	pub use report::{
12	    insert_wf_before, render_report, topics, underline_topic, WfError, WfReport, SUCCESS_LINE,
13	};
14	
15	/// Topics that follow the public-names report in the canonical order; the
16	/// public-names report is inserted immediately before the first of these that
17	/// is present in the report. (Unbound variables and Fresh public constants
18	/// precede the insertion point and are therefore NOT anchors.)
19	pub fn after_public_names_topics() -> Vec<&'static str> {
20	    vec![
21	        checks::T_SORTS,
22	        checks::T_RESERVED,
23	        checks::T_RESERVED_PREFIX,
24	        checks::T_FR,
25	        checks::T_SPECIAL,
26	        checks::T_FACT_CAP,
27	        checks::T_ARITY,
28	        checks::T_MULT,
29	        checks::T_LHSRHS,
30	        checks::T_LEFT,
31	        checks::T_RIGHT,
32	        checks::T_QUANT_SORTS,
33	        checks::T_FORMULA_TERMS,
34	        checks::T_GUARD,
35	        checks::T_LEMMA_ANNOT,
36	        checks::T_MULRESTRICT,
37	        checks::T_NAT,
38	        checks::T_SUBTERM,
39	    ]
40	}
41	
42	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order. The "Formula terms" check runs
43	/// in free-variable-only mode; for reducible-function coverage use
44	/// [`check_theory_with_reducible`].
45	pub fn check_theory(thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
46	    check_theory_with_reducible(thy, &std::collections::BTreeSet::new())
47	}
48	
49	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order, passing a caller-computed set
50	/// of reducible function-symbol names to the "Formula terms" check (theories
51	/// with `equations:` make some user functions reducible; the caller derives
52	/// that set - typically from the equation theory / Maude - and this pipeline
53	/// only consumes it).
54	pub fn check_theory_with_reducible(
55	    thy: &Theory,
56	    reducible: &std::collections::BTreeSet<String>,
57	) -> WfReport {
58	    let mut report: WfReport = Vec::new();
59	    // Base pipeline (public names inserted afterwards at its anchor point).
60	    report.extend(checks::unbound_variables(thy)); // 1
61	    report.extend(checks::fresh_public_constants(thy)); // 2
62	    report.extend(checks::mismatching_sorts(thy)); // 4
63	    report.extend(checks::reserved_names(thy)); // 5
64	    report.extend(checks::reserved_prefixes(thy)); // 6 (diff mode only)
65	    report.extend(checks::fr_facts(thy)); // 7
66	    report.extend(checks::special_facts(thy)); // 8
67	    report.extend(checks::fact_capitalization(thy)); // 8b (precedes arity)
68	    report.extend(checks::fact_arity(thy)); // 9
69	    report.extend(checks::fact_multiplicity(thy)); // 10
70	    report.extend(checks::fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs(thy)); // 11
71	    report.extend(checks::diff_left_right(thy)); // 12, 13 (diff mode only)
72	    // The Quantifier-sorts / Formula-terms / Formula-guardedness bundle is
73	    // emitted per formula item (lemmas then restrictions), interleaved by topic.
74	    report.extend(checks::formula_reports(thy, reducible)); // 14, 15, 16
75	    report.extend(checks::lemma_annotations(thy)); // 17
76	    report.extend(checks::multiplication_restriction(thy)); // 17
77	    report.extend(checks::nat_sorts(thy)); // 18
78	    report.extend(checks::subterm_convergence(thy)); // 19
79	
80	    let pn = checks::public_names_report(thy); // 3
81	    let anchors = after_public_names_topics();
82	    insert_wf_before(&mut report, pn, &anchors);
83	    report
84	}
85	
86	/// Secondary entry point: report any of `lemma_names` that are not declared in
87	/// the theory. NOTE: this entry point's exact render string could not be
88	/// observed through the file oracle; the set-difference logic is verified but
89	/// the message text is a documented gap (see BEHAVIOR.md).
90	pub fn check_if_lemmas_in_theory(lemma_names: &[String], thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
91	    let present = checks::theory_lemma_names(thy);
92	    let mut out = Vec::new();
93	    for name in lemma_names {
94	        if !present.contains(name) {
95	            out.push(WfError::new(
96	                "Lemmas",
97	                format!("lemma `{}' referenced but not present in theory", name),
98	            ));
99	        }
100	    }
101	    out
102	}

108	};

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Fast full-corpus WELLFORMEDNESS gate: diff the wf WARNING block of every
# corpus theory against the Haskell oracle.  The wf report is emitted at
# theory-load time, so this runs WITHOUT `--prove` (~1s/file vs minutes) —
# fast enough to run on every build.
#
# It reuses the batch gate's HS cache (scripts/.hs_file_cache, produced by
# corpus_file_diff.sh) for the reference side, extracting just the wf block.
# The RS side is whatever binary RS_PATH points at (default: the release
# build); a sealed-side harness that emits the same theory-load output can be
# gated the same way by setting RS_PATH.
#
# Env: RS_PATH, HS_CACHE (dir), JOBS, FILE_TIMEOUT, RESULTS_TSV, ALLOWLIST.
# Output TSV (3 col): relpath  MATCH|DIFF|SKIP_NO_HS  diffcount
set -u
export PATH="/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH"
echo 1000 > /proc/self/oom_score_adj 2>/dev/null || true
ulimit -v 25165824 2>/dev/null || true

script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)
repo_root=$(dirname "$script_dir")
RS_PATH="${RS_PATH:-$repo_root/target/release/tamarin-prover}"
HS_CACHE="${HS_CACHE:-$script_dir/.hs_file_cache}"
CORPUS_ROOT="${CORPUS_ROOT:-$repo_root/tamarin-prover/examples}"
FLAGS_MAP="${FLAGS_MAP:-$script_dir/file_flags.tsv}"
JOBS="${JOBS:-4}"
FILE_TIMEOUT="${FILE_TIMEOUT:-120}"
DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT="${DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT:-10}"
RESULTS_TSV="${RESULTS_TSV:-$script_dir/wf_gate_results.tsv}"
export RS_PATH HS_CACHE CORPUS_ROOT FLAGS_MAP FILE_TIMEOUT DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT
[ -x "$RS_PATH" ] || { echo "no RS binary at $RS_PATH" >&2; exit 2; }

strip_env() {
    grep -v -e '^Git revision:' -e '^Compiled at:' \
            -e '^[[:space:]]*processing time:' -e '^[[:space:]]*analyzed:'
}
# Isolate the wf report: either the success line, or the WARNING block that
# opens the leading theory comment (up to its closing `*/`).
wf_block() {
    awk '
        /^\/\* All wellformedness checks were successful\. \*\/$/ { print; next }
        /^WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!$/  { f=1 }
        f { print }
        f && /^\*\/$/ { f=0 }
    '
}
flags_for() { [ -f "$FLAGS_MAP" ] && awk -F'\t' -v r="$1" '!/^#/ && $1==r {print $2; exit}' "$FLAGS_MAP"; }
ckey() {

    if [ -n "$fl" ]; then printf '%s__f%s' "$h" "$(printf '%s' "$fl" | sha256sum | cut -c1-12)"

input.command:
cd /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs
export PATH="/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH"
CORPUS=tamarin-prover/examples
CACHE=scripts/.hs_file_cache
FLAGS=scripts/file_flags.tsv
flags_for(){ awk -F'\t' -v r="$1" '!/^#/ && $1==r {print $2; exit}' "$FLAGS" 2>/dev/null; }
exclude='stateverif_left_right|issue515|issue527|CertificateTransparency|OCSPS'
# For each corpus file, compute key, check cached footer; emit warning-carriers with line count
while IFS= read -r f; do
  rel=${f#$CORPUS/}
  echo "$rel" | grep -qE "$exclude" && continue
  h=$(sha256sum "$f" | cut -d' ' -f1); fl=$(flags_for "$rel")
  if [ -n "$fl" ]; then key="${h}__f$(printf '%s' "$fl" | sha256sum | cut -c1-12)"; else key="$h"; fi
  gz="$CACHE/$key.full.gz"
  [ -f "$gz" ] || continue
  n=$(zcat "$gz" 2>/dev/null | grep -oE "WARNING: ([0-9]+) wellformedness check failed" | grep -oE "[0-9]+" | head -1)
  [ -n "$n" ] && printf "%s\t%s\t%s\n" "$(wc -l < "$f")" "$n" "$rel"
done < <(find "$CORPUS" -name '*.spthy' | sort) | sort -n | head -25

# Open-side integration report — round-7 wellformedness re-sync, unit C
#   per-finding report granularity (batch WARNING count now HS-faithful)

Date: 2026-07-18. Integrator: open-side wf integrator (mechanical re-sync +
open-side assembly adjustment only; no logic transplanted, no hand-edit of the
sealed checker). Round 7 restructured the sealed wf-clean report-entry
granularity so the checker returns ONE `WfError` per INDIVIDUAL finding (rather
than one bundled `WfError` per topic block). This makes `wf_report.len()` — and
therefore the batch footer `WARNING: <N> wellformedness check failed!` — equal
the oracle's per-finding count, WITHOUT changing any rendered block byte.

## C.1 Re-sync of the round-7 clean sources — DONE
Re-applied the established mechanical recipe (`crate::{pretty,report,formula,
checks}` -> `super::…`; `crate::ast` kept — resolves to the real tamarin-parser
AST) to the round-7 `wf-clean/src`. TWO files changed vs the tree:
* `wf/report.rs` <- wf-clean/src/report.rs. New: `FINDING_SEP = "\n  \n"` and
  `group_findings`, which merge CONSECUTIVE same-topic findings into one
  `(topic, body)` block before rendering; `render_report` now groups first, so
  `report.len()` is the finding count while the rendered blocks stay byte-stable.
* `wf/checks.rs` <- wf-clean/src/checks.rs. The grouped-list checks switched
  from one bundled `WfError` (`entries.join("\n  \n")`) to `per_finding(topic,
  entries)` = one `WfError` per entry, and `formula_reports` dropped its
  `merge_consecutive` helper to emit the QS/FT/GUARD bundle per-item. Affected
  topics: Unbound variables, mismatching sorts (the "Possible reasons:" preamble
  rides the FIRST finding), Reserved names, Fr facts, Special facts, Nat Sorts,
  Fresh public constants, Reserved prefixes, Left/Right rule, Lemma annotations,
  Multiplication restriction, and the Quantifier-sorts / Formula-terms /
  Formula-guardedness bundle.
* `wf/formula.rs`, `wf/pretty.rs`, `wf/mod.rs` — reverse-transform BYTE-IDENTICAL
  to the round-7 sealed sources (unchanged since round-6).
PRESERVED workspace lines `pub mod order; pub use order::*;` + `wf/order.rs`
untouched. Fidelity: `sed 's/super::/crate::/g' <vendored>` reverse-maps
byte-identical to each sealed source (checks/formula/pretty/report). All six
`wf/` files remain headerless (0 `.hs` citations; tripwire clean).

## C.2 Open-side adjustment — pretty_theory::wf_headerless_preamble
The open side does NOT render via the sealed `report::render_report`; it renders
via `pretty_theory::format_wf_block` -> `render_wf_error_report`, which groups
the report by TOPIC (all same-topic entries together, first-appearance order)
and, for the header-less topics, joins their per-finding bodies with `"\n  \n"`.
That existing all-together grouping already reproduces the byte-exact block for
every per-finding topic registered in `wf_headerless_preamble` — i.e. every
corpus-exercised topic (Unbound variables, mismatching sorts, Reserved names,
Special facts, Quantifier sorts, Formula terms, Formula guardedness, Lemma
annotations). So NO change was needed for the gated topics: the granularity
change is byte-inert there and the footer count simply becomes per-finding.

ONE adjustment WAS required for seven header-less topics that were NOT registered
in `wf_headerless_preamble` and so fell through to the default (per-message
concatenation) path: `Fr facts must only use a fresh- or a msg-variable`,
`Fresh public constants`, `Nat Sorts`, `Multiplication restriction of rules`,
`Reserved prefixes`, `Left rule`, `Right rule`. Round-6 emitted each as ONE
bundled `WfError`, so the default path rendered it as a single body; round-7
emits N per-finding `WfError`s, which the default path would concatenate with a
plain `\n` (dropping the `"\n  \n"` finding separator) — a byte change on any
theory with >=2 findings in one of these topics. Registered the seven in
`wf_headerless_preamble` returning an EMPTY preamble, so their per-finding bodies
take the same `"\n  \n"` join path as the other header-less topics. Verified
byte-identical to the round-6 render on a 2-rule fresh-public-constants probe
(block unchanged; footer count 1 -> 2).

KEEP-AND-REPORT (pre-existing, NOT introduced this round): these same seven
topics carry NO underline header in the RS render (round-6 already emitted the
body with no `underlineTopic` line; the HS oracle DOES underline them, per the
`r5_freshpub_prove` / `round2_multiplication_in_rule_lhs` framing captures).
NONE of the seven appears in the 419-file wf corpus (verified: 0 files each), so
the gate never exercised the gap and could not this round either. The
empty-preamble registration deliberately preserves the round-6 bytes (no header)
rather than inventing one, since the exact HS header format is unverifiable
through the gate for five of the seven (only fresh-pub and mult-restriction have
a captured reference). Flagged for a future sealed round to emit the header.

`RESULTS_TSV=scripts/wf_gate_round7.tsv JOBS=6 bash scripts/wf_gate.sh`, RS =
